message:
perf(combinator): replace O(n) Vec::remove with VecDeque tombstone GC in Bulkhead queue

The Bulkhead queue used Vec::remove(idx) in claim_entry() and
cancel_entry(), causing O(n) shifts for every dequeue operation. Under
high contention with many queued operations, this quadratic behavior
degraded throughput.

Replace the Vec<QueueEntry> with VecDeque<QueueEntry> and switch from
eager removal to a tombstone-and-GC pattern:

- Add RejectionReason::Consumed variant as the tombstone marker
- claim_entry(): Mark consumed entries with Consumed instead of removing
- cancel_entry(): Mark entries with Consumed and read result/weight
  before tombstoning, then drop the lock before releasing permits
- process_queue_inner(): GC tombstones from the front of the deque
  before processing timeouts and grants
- enqueue(): GC tombstones before checking capacity to prevent false
  queue-full rejections from accumulated tombstones
- Handle double-poll of Consumed entries gracefully (return Cancelled)

This changes all queue mutations from O(n) to amortized O(1), since
tombstones are only ever popped from the front.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
